title: قرار مجلس ادارة الهيئة الاتحادية للرقابة النووية رقم 17: بالموافقة على الشروط المرجعية المُعدَّلة للجنة الوقاية من الإشعاعات
short_name: قرار مجلس ادارة الهيئة الاتحادية للرقابة النووية 17/2021

provisions.title: قررمايلي
provisions.content: من القانونالتي نصت علىصلاحية مجلسإدارة الهيئةبتشكيل "لجنةالوقاية منالإشعاعات فيالدولة"وتحديدصلاحياتاللجنة ونظامعملها؛-قرار مجلسإدارة الهيئةرقم (15) لسنة 2021بالتعديل رقم 4للائحةالداخليةالمنظِّمةلاجتماعاتمجلس إدارةالهيئةالاتحاديةللرقابةالنووية؛-قرار مجلسإدارة الهيئةرقم (4) لسنة 2011بتشكيل لجنةالوقاية منالإشعاعات فيالدولةوتحديداختصاصاتهاونظامعملها؛-قرار مجلسإدارة الهيئةرقم (8) لسنة 2014بتجديد مدةعمل لجنةالوقاية منالإشعاعات؛-قرار مجلسإدارة الهيئةرقم (5) لسنة 2016بشأن إعادةتشكيل لجنةالوقاية منالإشعاعات فيالدولةوإصدارالشروطالمرجعيةللجنةالوقاية منالإشعاعات؛-قرار مجلسإدارة الهيئةرقم (17) لسنة 2019بتجديد مدةعمل لجنةالوقاية منالإشعاعات فيالدولةوإعتمادشروطهاالمرجعيةالمُعدَّلة؛وبناءًعلى توصيةمدير عامالهيئة، وعلىموافقة مجلسإدارة الهيئةخلال اجتماعهرقم (3) لسنة 2021المنعقد في 19مايو 2021،قررمايلي :المادةالأولىالموافقةعلى الشروطالمرجعيةالمُعدَّلةللجنةالوقاية منالإشعاعات فيالدولة حسبماهو وارد فيالملحق (1) بهذاالقرار.

provisions.title: الموافق:7 شوال 1442هـ
provisions.content:
يدخلهذا القرارحيز التنفيذاعتباراً منتاريخ إصدارهويُنشر فيالجريدةالرسمية.صدرفيأبوظبي
بتاريخ:19 مايو2021
الموافق:7 شوال 1442هـعبداللهالسويديرئيسمجلسالإدارةنشرهذا القرار فيعدد الجريدةالرسمية رقم705.ص61.ResolutionNo. (17) of 2021 of the Board of Management of the Federal Authority for NuclearRegulationApprovingthe Amended Terms of Reference of the Radiation ProtectionCommitteeTheBoard of Management of the Federal Authority for Nuclear Regulation("FANR"):Havingconsidered:-The Federal Law by Decree No. (6) of 2009, Concerning the Peaceful Uses ofNuclear Energy (the ’’Law");-Cabinet Resolution No. (17/1 g) of 2021 on Renewal of Formation of the Board ofManagement of the Federal Authority for Nuclear Regulation;-Cabinet Resolution No. (2/9g) of 2020 on the Guide to Board Governance in theUAE Federal Government;-Provisions of Article (67) of the Law envisaging the authority of the FANR'sBoard of Management to establish "the Radiation Protection Committee in theState" and to specify authorities and bylaws of such committee;-FANR's Board of Management Resolution No. (15) of 2021 on Amendment No. 4 of theBy-Laws Regulating the Meetings of the Board of Management of the FederalAuthority for Nuclear Regulation;-FANR’s Board of Management Resolution No. (4) of 2011 Establishing theRadiation Protection Committee in the State and determining its Authorities andWork Policy;-FANR's Board of Management Resolution No. (8) of 2014 on Renewal of the Term ofthe Radiation Protection Committee;-FANR's Board of Management Resolution No. (5) of 2016 concerning Re-Formation ofthe Radiation Protection Committee in the State and Issuance of the Terms ofReference of the Radiation Protection Committee;-FANR's Board of Management Resolution No. (17) of 2019 Renewing the Term of theRadiation Protection Committee in the State and Approving its Amended Terms ofReference; andBasedon the recommendation of the Director General of FANR and on FANR's Board ofManagement approval during its meeting No. (3) of 2021 held on 19 May2021,Hasdecided the following:Article1Toapprove the Amended Terms of Reference of the Radiation Protection Committee inthe State, as provided in Annex 1 to this Resolution.Article2ThisResolution shall come into force as of the date of its issuance and shall bepublished in the Official Gazette.AbdullaAl Suwaidi ChairmanIssuedin Abu Dhabi
Date: 7 Shawwal 1442 AH
Corresponding to: 19 May 2021ANNEX1:AMENDEDTERMS OF REFERENCE OF THE RADIATION PROTECTION COMMITTEE IN THE STATE1-Introduction1.1-Pursuant to Article 67 of the Federal Law by Decree No. 6 of 2009 Concerning thePeaceful Uses of Nuclear Energy, “[t]he Board shall establish, by adecision, a committee to be named 'the Radiation Protection Committee in theState’, the decision shall specify the authorities and bylaws of suchcommittee”.1.2-On 20 July 2011, the Board of Management of the Federal Authority for NuclearRegulation (FANR) issued Resolution No. (4) of 2011 Establishing the RadiationProtection Committee in the State and Determining its Authorities and WorkPolicy. The Radiation Protection Committee in the State was established for aperiod of three (3) years. On 14 July 2014, the Board of Management of FANRissued Resolution No. (8) of 2014 for the Renewal of the Term of the RadiationProtection Committee.1.3-The Board of Management on 16 August 2016 issued the Resolution No. (5) of 2016Concerning Re-Formation of the Radiation Protection Committee in the State andIssuance of the Terms of Reference of the Radiation Protection Committee. By aResolution No. (17) of 2019, the Board of Management renewed the Term of theRadiation Protection Committee for another period of three (3) years and amendedthe Terms of Reference.2-Purpose of the Terms of Reference - Amendments2.1-The purpose of the Terms of Reference (the ‘ToR’) of the RadiationProtection Committee in the State (the ‘Committee’) is to define theobjectives and scope of the work and functions of the Committee; itscomposition, the meetings and quorum; recommendations and voting;confidentiality; reporting; and responsibilities of its members, the Chair andSecretary.2.3-Amendments to the ToR of the Committee shall be approved by the Board ofManagement of FANR upon recommendation of the Chair of the Committee.3-Scope and Functions of the Committee3.1-The Committee shall provide advice and consultation to FANR management and toother relevant authorities including the General Secretariat of the Cabinet onmatters related to radiation protection in medical, occupational and publicexposure, and environmental radiological protection, emergency preparedness andresponse, education and training, research and development to enhance theradiation protection in the State.3.2-Within its scope, the Committee shall carry out the following functions:a)work with the competent entities in the State to develop national policies,strategies, guidance and programs on radiation protection related matters indifferent exposure situations to be implemented in the UAE, and to update thesame when required;b)determine the education, training and qualification requirements in the State inthe radiation protection field and liaise with the competent, federal and localentities for implementation;c)adopt recommendations in the areas envisaged in paragraph 3.1 of the ToR whichshall be applicable to the entities represented in the Committee (theRepresented Authorities), as identified in paragraph 4.2 of the ToR and to otherrelevant authorities as may be necessary;d)provide advice to relevant authorities on the means to increase awarenessamongst users of radiation sources of good practices in radiation protection andsafety culture;e)approve formation of permanent working groups consisting of experts of theRepresented Authorities, approve their terms of reference, and establish, asrequired, task forces to consider specific topics;f)consider the recommendations formulated by the working groups or task forces,and as needed provide guidance on the way forward to achieve specific workinggroups or task forces objectives;g)monitor the developments in the international system of Radiation Protection andanalyse them with the view of their reflection in the UAE regulatoryframework;h)prepare proposals for the UAE Cabinet on radiation protection related issues ofa national relevance, following the applicable guidelines established by theCabinet;i)review implementation of the ToR and, when necessary, propose amendments to theChair of the Committee for submission to the Board of Management for approval;andj)carry out any other functions the Board of Management of FANR assigns toit.4-Composition of the Committee4.1-The Committee is formed for a period of three (3) years subject to renewal bythe Board of Management of FANR, upon request of the Chair of theCommittee.4.2-The following entities shall be represented in the Committee (the‘Represented Authorities’) and each shall nominate one (1)representative at a senior level as a member of the Committee:NumberRepresentedAuthority1FederalAuthority for Nuclear Regulation2TheArmed Forces3Ministryof Interior4Ministryof Health and Prevention5Ministryof Climate Change & Environment6EmiratesNuclear Energy Corporation7FederalCustoms Authority8KhalifaUniversity9Departmentof Health - Abu Dhabi10DubaiHealth Authority11EnvironmentAgency - Abu Dhabi12NationalEmergency, Crisis and Disasters Management Authority13GeneralAuthority for the Security of Ports, Borders and Free Zones14NawahEnergy Company15AbuDhabi Center for Public Health16AbuDhabi National Oil Company (ADNOC)4.3-The Chair of the Committee shall be the Director General of FANR. The DeputyChair, who shall be the Director of the Department of Radiation Safety of FANR,shall replace the Chair in his or her absence.4.4-Each Represented Authority shall have the right to nominate up to three (3)observers. The observers shall have no right to participate in the decisions ofthe Committee.4.5-Nomination(s) of representatives of the Represented Authorities and observers tothe Committee, and any subsequent changes in such nomination(s), shall be sentby each Represented Authority to the Chair of the Committee in a timely mannerbefore a meeting of the Committee.4.6-The members of the Committee shall:a)Be committed to prepare for and attend the Committee meetings;b)Contribute actively to the work of the Committee and provide a feedback on thedocuments prepared by the Committee, its working groups or taskforces; andc)Propose for consideration any topic related to the areas envisaged in paragraph3 of theToR.4.7-If a Committee member is absent in two (2) consecutive Committee meetings orthree (3) intermittent meetings during the year, the Chair of the Committeeshall send a notification to the relevant Represented Authority to takenecessary actions in this respect.5.Quorum and MeetingsQuorum5.1A meeting of the Committee shall not be valid unless attended by the majority ofits members including the Chair of the Committee (or, in his or her absence, theDeputy Chair).Meetings5.2The Committee shall meet at least once every three (3) months or whenevernecessary at the invitation of the Chair of the Committee (or, in his or herabsence, the Deputy Chair).5.3The Chair of the Committee shall:a)circulate meeting invitations to the members and observers of the Committee andprovide a meeting agenda, summary of the subjects and draft recommendations tobe deliberated upon (such invitations and accompanying documents shall becirculated at least one (1) week before the meeting);b)invite, as may be required, experts to the meetings of the Committee to assistthe Committee in its work;c)receive the nominations of the representative from each Represented Authority inthe Committee and monitor the attendance of the Represented Authorities in themeetings of the Committee;d)submit to the Committee the terms of reference of the working groups envisagedin paragraph 3.2(e) of the ToR;e)monitor the implementation of the Committee recommendations;f)submit periodic reports to the Board of Management of FANR on therecommendations of the Committee; andg)Appoint the Secretary of the Committee from FANR’s employees.5.4-Upon request of the Board of Management of FANR, the Deputy Chair of theCommittee shall report on the activities of the Committee.5.5-The members of the working groups shall:a)send recommendations to the Committee within their remit as defined in theworking groups terms of reference approved by the Committee;b) develop a Program for implementing the objectives as specified in their termsof reference within three months of the working group establishment and reviseit on a regular basis.5.6-The Represented Authorities shall bear all costs and expenses incurred by theirrepresentatives and observer(s) for participation in the meetings and activitiesof the Committee.6-Decisions and Voting6.1-The Committee shall approve its recommendations and the terms of reference ofthe working groups by consensus of the members present. In case there is noconsensus, recommendations shall be immediately put to the vote and consideredadopted in case of a simple majority of affirmative votes of the members presentand voting.6.2-In the event of equal votes (i.e. a tie), the Chair (or, in his or her absence,the Deputy Chair) of the Committee shall have a casting vote and the side of theChair shall be considered as the side with the greater number of votes infulfillment of the requirements envisaged in Article 6.1 of these ToR.6.3-Objecting members shall have the right to register their objections includingthe reason for objections in the minutes of the meeting.6.4The expert(s) invited by the Chair of the Committee and observers nominated bythe Represented Authorities to attend meetings of the Committee shall have noright to participate in the decisions of the Committee.6.5-Approved recommendations shall be forwarded to the Board of Management of FANRfor information, endorsement or action, as appropriate.7-Con
